recovery_prompt: Connection refused by the server. Try: (1) verify the server is running, (2) check the host and port, (3) check firewall rules, (4) try increasing the timeout, (5) verify the service isn't overloaded.

DNS resolution failed. Try: (1) check the hostname spelling, (2) try with an IP address instead, (3) check /etc/hosts, (4) verify DNS server configuration, (5) try a different DNS resolver (e.g., 8.8.8.8).

SSL/TLS handshake failed. Try: (1) update CA certificates, (2) check for certificate expiry, (3) verify the hostname matches the certificate, (4) check TLS version compatibility, (5) for development only: disable verification (never in production).
Network request timed out. Try: (1) increase the timeout value, (2) check network connectivity, (3) retry with exponential backoff, (4) check for server overload, (5) reduce request payload size.

Connection was reset by the remote peer. Try: (1) retry with backoff, (2) check if the server has rate limiting, (3) verify keep-alive settings, (4) check for load balancer timeouts, (5) implement connection pooling.

A required Python module is missing. Try: (1) install it with 'pip install <module>', (2) check the module name spelling, (3) verify you're in the correct virtual environment, (4) check PYTHONPATH, (5) try 'pip install -r requirements.txt'.

Cannot import a name from a module. Try: (1) check if the name exists in the module's __all__, (2) the module version may differ—check with pip show, (3) look for API changes, (4) try importing the module directly.
Circular import detected. Try: (1) move the shared code to a separate module, (2) use lazy imports (import inside function), (3) restructure module boundaries, (4) use TYPE_CHECKING for type hints.

pattern: shared object|\.so[:\.]|cannot open shared object file|Library not loaded
recovery_prompt: A native shared library dependency is missing. Try: (1) install the system package (apt/brew), (2) set LD_LIBRARY_PATH, (3) reinstall the Python package, (4) check for version compatibility.

Type mismatch in operation. Try: (1) check types of operands, (2) add explicit type conversion (str(), int(), float()), (3) handle None cases, (4) add type checking/assertions, (5) check for mixed types in collections.

Object missing expected attribute. Try: (1) check if the object is None, (2) verify the object type, (3) use hasattr() or getattr() with defaults, (4) check for typos in attribute names, (5) check class initialization.

Dictionary key not found. Try: (1) use dict.get(key, default) instead of dict[key], (2) check the key name (typos, case sensitivity), (3) verify the data structure matches expectations, (4) add key existence checks.

error_message: IndexError list index out of range
recovery_prompt: List index is out of range. Try: (1) check list length before indexing, (2) use try/except IndexError, (3) verify loop bounds, (4) check for off-by-one errors, (5) use slicing for safe access.
Value conversion error. Try: (1) validate input before conversion, (2) use try/except around int()/float(), (3) strip whitespace from input, (4) handle empty strings, (5) add input validation.

Function received an unexpected keyword argument. Try: (1) check the function signature, (2) look for API changes, (3) check for typos in argument names, (4) verify the function version matches documentation.
Function call is missing required arguments. Try: (1) check the function signature, (2) verify all positional args are provided, (3) check for API changes, (4) use keyword arguments for clarity.

pattern: too many values to unpack|not enough values to unpack|unpack
recovery_prompt: Unpacking mismatch. Try: (1) check the number of variables vs iterable length, (2) use *args to capture extra values, (3) verify the data structure shape, (4) use .items() for dict unpacking.

An unexpected error occurred. Try: (1) read the full error message and traceback, (2) check recent changes, (3) add logging for debugging, (4) isolate the failing code, (5) search for the error message online.

A runtime error occurred. Try: (1) read the full error message, (2) check for resource exhaustion, (3) verify environment configuration, (4) check for race conditions, (5) add error handling around the failing code.

Feature not implemented. Try: (1) implement the missing method/function, (2) use an alternative approach, (3) check for a library that provides this feature, (4) file an issue if it's expected to work.

Out of memory. Try: (1) process data in smaller batches, (2) use streaming instead of loading all data, (3) close unused resources, (4) add garbage collection hints, (5) increase available memory or use a machine with more RAM.

Maximum recursion depth exceeded. Try: (1) convert to iterative approach, (2) increase sys.setrecursionlimit() if legitimate, (3) check for infinite recursion bugs, (4) use tail recursion optimization.

Process was interrupted. Try: (1) add signal handling for graceful shutdown, (2) save state periodically for resume capability, (3) use checkpointing for long-running tasks, (4) clean up resources in finally blocks.
